query: low resolution pixelated mosaic blocky digital image
message: sub:digital_artifacts/pixelation — anti_aesthetic — pixel mosaic, blocky low-res, color-cell grids

response.objective_caption: An image photographed through a dark wire mesh screen showing several circular light shapes in white and blue tones arranged horizontally across a black background. The mesh creates a uniform grid of small rectangles covering the entire frame. The lights vary in size and brightness, with some appearing to overlap, visible as rounded illuminated forms behind the dark grille.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: An abstract photograph taken through a dark mesh screen, intentionally employing blur to render background lights as soft white and blue orbs against a black void. The image abandons representational clarity in favor of pure shape, color, and texture, with the uniform grid of the screen adding a tactile geometric layer over the unfocused circular light forms. Through shallow depth of field, the light sources dissolve into ambiguous glowing spheres, making the composition prioritize formal pattern and mood over identifiable subject matter.
